'''Holocron of King Adas''',<ref name="TEGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> also known as '''Adas' holocron''' was the original [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], made by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Adas]] around [[27,700 BBY]]. He learned how to create the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] from the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] who had come to [[Invasion of Korriban|conquer]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and whom he had driven off.<ref name="END" />

==History==
After its creation, Adas's holocron passed down through the years until, around the time of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Garu]] lost the holocron on [[Ashas Ree/Legends|Ashas Ree]]. Centuries later the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fallen Jedi]] [[Freedon Nadd]] found it, and used its dark knowledge to conquer [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. It was eventually uncovered by [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], who learned all its stored secrets. The holocron was then passed to her apprentice [[Novar (Human)|Novar]], to teach [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] initiates. The [[Gatekeeper/Legends|Gatekeeper]] of Adas's holocron, however, had a unique personality that could turn down the user's request or even threaten its user. Amanoa and the [[Naddist]]s were defeated, and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] thereafter hid Adas's holocron in a geyser crater beneath the deep waters of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kodai]].<ref name="END" />

In [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]], [[Norval]], a student of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] university [[Professor/Legends|professor]] [[Murk Lundi]], following instructions, retrieved Adas's holocron, which had been discovered and afterwards held briefly by his teacher before it was lost again in the depths of Kodai's seas, when the two Sith fanatics fought over it.<ref name="FOLLOWERS">''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: The Followers]]''</ref> A decade later, in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] (''alias'' Darth Sidious) secretly sent [[Carnor Jax]]'s father, a [[Sun Guard]] of [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]], to retrieve the holocron—which was now en route to [[Ploo II/Legends|Ploo II]] from Kodai, Norval having at last recovered the holocron by his own devices; Sidious killed the secret agent from Thyrsus when he failed to reclaim the ancient artifact.<ref name="END" /> Also pursuing Norval, however, were [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who were successful at last in reclaiming Adas's holocron from the fanatical Sith aspirant's possession; the ancient receptacle of forbidden knowledge was then taken to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] by order of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], delivered to chief archivist [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]], and secured in a safe within the Temple archive vaults.<ref name="FOLLOWERS" />

The Council, for reasons that remain unclear, later sent Adas's holocron to Jedi-affiliated [[House Pelagia]]'s [[Holocron library]].<ref name="TEGTTF" /> The infamous artifact was subsequently [[Mecetti Purge|stolen]] from the library by Sir [[Nevil Tritum]] of the [[Mecrosa Order]] during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="END" /> Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the Mecrosa Order gave the Sith holocron to [[Lumiya]]. It is unknown whether the holocron was still in her possession when she died in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].

That same year, Jedi historian [[Tionne Solusar]] incorrectly stated in her [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force|records]] that [[Norval]] found Adas's holocron in 29 BBY, instead of 39 BBY—a statement, however, which isn't wholly incorrect, as the Sith artifact, while initially uncovered on the earlier date, was not in fact physically recovered and retained by Norval until 29 BBY.<ref name="TEGTTF" />
